title: Interpret trigonometric graphs in context
description: Analyze real-world situations that are modeled by a trigonometric function.

assessment_items.question_content:
A piece of gum is stuck to the tire of a bike wheel in motion. The vertical distance between the gum and the ground, in centimeters, is modeled by $H(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **midline**!


[[☃ image 1]]

We use [[☃ definition 1]] models to show cycles. In this case, the cycle is from the circular motion of the gum stuck to the bike's wheel. The midline tells us the value at the middle of the cycle. What is exactly at the middle between the highest and lowest points the piece of gum reaches?

These are the answers:

1. Midline
2. The wheel's center is $30$ centimeters above the ground.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled H on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by ten. The t axis scales by one. The wave starts at its maximum at (zero, sixty). The other maximums are at (two, sixty), (four, sixty), (six, sixty), and (eight, sixty). The minimums are at (one, zero), (three, zero), (five, zero), and (seven, zero). There is a horizontal highlighted dashed line at y equals thirty.

choices.content: The bike's speed is $30$ centimeters per second.
The diameter of the wheel is $30$ centimeters.
The wheel's center is $30$ centimeters above the ground.
The wheel completes a single cycle in $30$ seconds.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
Emile is observing a wind turbine. The vertical distance between the ground and the tip of one of the turbine's blades, in meters, is modeled by $H(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **midline**!


[[☃ image 1]]

We use [[☃ definition 1]] models to show cycles. In this case, the cycle is from the circular motion of the blades on the turbine. The midline tells us the value at the middle of the cycle. What is exactly at the middle between the highest and lowest points the tip of the blade reaches?

These are the answers:

1. Midline
2. The turbine's center is $35$ meters above the ground.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled H on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by five. The t axis scales by ten. The wave starts at its midline at (zero, thirty five). The range of the wave is from twenty five to forty five. There is a horizontal highlighted dashed line at y equals thirty five.

choices.content: The length of the blade is $35$ meters.
The turbine has $35$ blades.
The turbine's center is $35$ meters above the ground.
The turbine completes a single cycle in $35$ seconds.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
Claire is swinging from the tip of a branch of a big tree. The horizontal distance between Claire and the trunk of the tree, in meters, is modeled by $D(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.


[[☃ image 1]]

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **midline**!


[[☃ image 1]]

We use [[☃ definition 1]] models to show cycles. In this case, the cycle is from Claire's repeated swinging back and forth. The midline tells us the value at the middle of the cycle. What is exactly at the middle between Claire's shortest and longest distances from the trunk?

These are the answers:

1. Midline
2. The tip of the branch is $2$ meters from the trunk.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled D on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by one half. The t axis scales by one. There is a horizontal highlighted dashed line at y equals two that is an equal distance from both the maximum and the minimum.

choices.content: The tip of the branch is $2$ meters from the trunk.
Claire completes a cycle every $2$ seconds.
Claire completes $2$ cycles per second.
The furthest Claire gets from the tip of the branch is $2$ meters.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
Cody is observing a roulette turning. The horizontal distance between Cody and the number 0 in the roulette, in meters, is modeled by $D(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **midline**!


[[☃ image 1]]

We use [[☃ definition 1]] models to show cycles. In this case, the cycle is from the circular motion of the roulette. The midline tells us the value at the middle of the cycle. What is exactly at the middle between Cody's shortest and longest distances from the number 0?

These are the answers:

1. Midline
2. Cody is $1.5$ meters from the center of the roulette.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled H on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by one half. The t axis scales by one. The wave starts at its minimum at (zero, one). The maximums are at (one half, two), (one and one half, two), (two and one half, two), (three and one half, two), and (four and one half, two). The other minimums are at (one, one), (two, one), (three, one), (four, one), and (five, one). There is a horizontal highlighted dashed line at y equals one and one half.

choices.content: Cody is $1.5$ meters from the center of the roulette.
The roulette completes a cycle every $1.5$ seconds.
The radius of the roulette is $1.5$ meters.
The diameter of the roulette is $1.5$ meters.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
Hugo has a pin attached to one of the spokes of his bike's wheel, for decoration. The height of the pin above the ground, in centimeters, is modeled by $H(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **amplitude**!


[[☃ image 1]]

We use [[☃ definition 1]] models to show cycles. In this case, the cycle is the pin's circular motion due to being attached to a bike wheel. The amplitude tells us the difference between the value at the middle of the cycle and the values at the extremes of the cycle. What would we call the difference between the pin's middle distance from the ground and its longest (or shortest) distance from the ground?

These are the answers:

1. Amplitude
2. The pin is $20$ centimeters from the center of the wheel.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled H on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by five. The t axis scales by one. The wave starts at its minimum at (zero, ten). The range of the wave is from ten to fifty. There is a highlighted vertical arrow that extends from the midline at y equals thirty to the maximum at (one and one half, fifty).

choices.content: The pin is $20$ centimeters from the center of the wheel.
The diameter of the wheel is $20$ centimeters.
The height of the wheel's center is $20$ centimeters.
The wheel completes a single cycle in $20$ seconds.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
Adan is observing a watermill. The vertical distance between the water level and the tip of one of the mill's blades, in meters, is modeled by $H(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds (negative values mean the tip is underwater). The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **amplitude**!

[[☃ image 1]]

We use [[☃ definition 1]] models to show cycles. In this case, the cycle is the circular motion of the blades on the mill. The amplitude tells us the difference between the value at the middle of the cycle and the values at the extremes of the cycle. What would we call the difference between the blade tip's middle height and its lowest (or highest) point?

These are the answers:

1. Amplitude
2. The length of the blade is $8$ meters.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled H on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by two. The t axis scales by ten. The wave starts at its midline at (zero, four). The maximums are at (ten, twelve), (fifty, twelve), and (ninety, twelve). The minimums are at (thirty, negative four) and (seventy, negative four). There is a highlighted vertical arrow that touches from the minimum to the midline.

choices.content: The length of the blade is $8$ meters.
The mill completes $8$ cycles in a second.
The mill's center is $8$ meters above the ground.
The mill completes a single cycle in $8$ seconds.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
Sia is swinging from a chandelier. The horizontal distance between Sia and the wall, in meters, is modeled by $D(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **amplitude**!


[[☃ image 1]]

We use [[☃ definition 1]] models to show cycles. In this case, the cycle is Sia's repeated swinging back and forth. The amplitude tells us the difference between the value at the middle of the cycle and the values at the extremes of the cycle. What would we call the difference between Sia's middle distance from the wall and her shortest (or longest) distance from the wall?

These are the answers:

1. Amplitude
2. The furthest Sia gets from the point where the chandelier hangs is $2$ meters.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled D on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by one. The t axis scales by one. The wave starts at its midline at (zero, five). The range of the wave is from three to seven. There is a highlighted vertical arrow that extends from the minimum to the midline at y equals five.

choices.content: The chandelier is hung $2$ meters from the wall.
The chandelier is $2$ meters wide.
Sia completes $2$ swing cycles per second.
The furthest Sia gets from the point where the chandelier hangs is $2$ meters.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
Maci is observing her younger brother riding a carousel. The horizontal distance between Maci and her brother, in meters, is modeled by $D(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **amplitude**!


[[☃ image 1]]

We use [[☃ definition 1]] models to show cycles. In this case, the cycle is the circular motion of Maci's brother on the carousel. The amplitude tells us the difference between the value at the middle of the cycle and the values at the extremes of the cycle. What would we call the difference between Maci's brother's middle distance from Maci and his longest (or shortest) distance from Maci?

These are the answers:

1. Amplitude
2. Maci's brother is $4$ meters from the center of the carousel.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled D on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by two. The t axis scales by ten. The wave starts at the midline at (zero, ten), travels down to (five, six), curves up through (ten, ten) to (fifteen, fourteen). It continues the pattern of curving up and down until it reaches a point on the midline at (ninety, ten). Its highest points are at (fifteen, fourteen), (thirty-five, fourteen), (fifty-five, fourteen), and (seventy-five, fourteen). Its lowest points are (five, six), (twenty-five, six), (forty-five, six), (sixty-five, six), and (eighty-five, six). A vertical line with arrows on the end extend from (thirty-five, ten) to (thirty-five, fourteen).

choices.content: Maci is $4$ meters from the center of the carousel.
The carousel completes $4$ cycles every second.
The carousel completes one cycle every $4$ seconds.
Maci's brother is $4$ meters from the center of the carousel.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
A fly is standing on a bike wheel in motion. The vertical distance between the fly and the ground, in centimeters, is modeled by $H(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **period**!


[[☃ image 1]]

What does it mean in terms of our context? Think about it this way: We see that the fly is at the top of the wheel at $t=1$ second, and then it reaches the top of the wheel again at $t=5$ seconds.

These are the answers:

1. Period
2. The wheel completes a single cycle in $4$ seconds.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled H on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by five. The t axis scales by one. A highlighted arrow points from one maximum part of the wave to the maximum next to it.

choices.content: The radius of the wheel is $4$ centimeters.
The bike's speed is $4$ centimeters per second.
The height of the wheel's center is $4$ centimeters.
The wheel completes a single cycle in $4$ seconds.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
Oliver is observing a windmill. The vertical distance between the ground and the tip of one of the mill's blades, in meters, is modeled by $H(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **period**!


[[☃ image 1]]

What does it mean in terms of our context? Think about it this way: We see that the tip of the blade reaches the lowest point at $t=10$ seconds, and then it reaches the lowest point again at $t=20$ seconds.

These are the answers:

1. Period
2. The mill completes a single cycle in $10$ seconds.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled H on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by five. The t axis scales by one. A highlighted arrow points from one minimum part of the wave to the minimum next to it.

choices.content: The length of the blade is $10$ meters.
The mill completes $10$ cycles in a second.
The mill has $10$ blades.
The mill completes a single cycle in $10$ seconds.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
Kai is swinging on a trapeze in a circus show. The horizontal distance between Kai and the edge of the stage, in meters, is modeled by $D(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **period**!

[[☃ image 1]]

What does it mean in terms of our context? Think about it this way: We see that Kai is furthest from the edge of the stage at $t=5$ seconds, and then he reaches the furthest point again at $t=15$ seconds.

These are the answers:

1. Period
2. Kai completes a single swing cycle in $10$ seconds.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled D on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by three. The t axis scales by five. A highlighted arrow points from one maximum part of the wave to the maximum next to it.

choices.content: The trapeze is hung $10$ meters from the edge of the stage.
The trapeze is $10$ meters long.
Kai completes $10$ swing cycles per second.
Kai completes a single swing cycle in $10$ seconds.
Midline
Amplitude
Period

assessment_items.question_content:
A ballerina is spinning while standing on one leg, so the point that is furthest from the center of her body is her other knee. The horizontal distance between the ballerina's knee and the edge of the stage, in meters, is modeled by $D(t)$ where $t$ is the time in seconds. The function is graphed below, along with one segment highlighted.

[[☃ image 1]]

***Answer two questions about the highlighted segment.***

**1) Which feature of the graph corresponds to the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

**2) What is the meaning of the highlighted segment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\maroonD{\text{Midline}}$ is the horizontal line that passes exactly between the minimum value and the maximum value of a sinusoidal graph.

$\greenD{\text{Amplitude}}$ is the vertical distance between the midline and any of the extremum values.

$\purpleC{\text{Period}}$ is the horizontal length of an entire cycle. A cycle of a sinusoidal graph can begin at any point, but common points to measure the cycle are between consecutive maximum points or between consecutive minimum points.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32f326608c39940c1e16a0d4ca7ec2722300a7b0)

So the highlighted segment is the graph's **period**!


[[☃ image 1]]

What does it mean in terms of our context? Think about it this way: We see that the ballerina's knee is closest to the edge of the stage at $t=0.4$ seconds, and then it reaches the closest point again at $t=0.8$ seconds.

These are the answers:

1. Period
2. The ballerina completes one spin in $0.4$ seconds.

options.alt: A trigonometric wave labeled D on a t y coordinate plane. The y-axis scales by one half. The t axis scales by one. A highlighted arrow points from one minimum part of the wave to the minimum next to it.

choices.content: The center of the ballerina's body is $0.4$ meters from the edge of the stage.
The center of the ballerina's body is $0.4$ meters from the ballerina's knee.
The ballerina completes one spin in $0.4$ seconds.
The ballerina completes $0.4$ spins in one second.
Midline
Amplitude
Period
